channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Predicting what 2025 will mean for AI
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 could borrow two great features from the Pixel 9 Pro
item.description: The next Samsung flagship could lift a couple of neat features from the Pixel 9 Pro, such as crash detection and a free trial of Gemini Advanced.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Nearly a year since launch, Apple’s Vision Pro still searching for a killer app
item.description: Apple launched its highly anticipated Vision Pro “spatial computing” headset last Feb. 2 amid significant hype — and hopes it could finally push virtual or mixed reality into mainstream use.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What to know about the car-renting app used in the Las Vegas and New Orleans attacks
item.description: The car-renting app Turo is in the spotlight after both the cars used in the New Orleans and Las Vegas attacks on New Year's Day were rented through the company.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: 12 must-have apps to crush your New Year’s resolutions
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you crush your New Year's goals with these 12 apps to help you stay on track and succeed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple could launch the iPhone SE 4 as the iPhone 16E, according to rumors
item.description: The handset previously known as the iPhone SE 4 could in fact become the iPhone 16E, according to leaks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Germany's Scholz takes veiled swipe at Musk in New Year's address
item.description: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz offered subtle criticism around billionaire Elon Musk's influence on President-elect Trump early Tuesday, saying the nation's fate will not be decided by "the owners of social media.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: The best new Windows 11 features of 2024
item.description: Microsoft is a little sneaky. Sure, there’s just one “big” update for Windows 11 each year. But Microsoft’s Windows team is always working on something, and new Windows 11 features are arriving on PCs every month — even outside of those high-profile updates.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Honey browser extension accused of scamming influencers, users
item.description: In the the 23-minute video by user MegaLag, the PayPal property is accused of abusing the affiliate link system for its own profit.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Ford’s X account calls Israel a ‘terrorist state’ in apparent hack incident
item.description: The official account for Ford Motor Co. on the social platform X was apparently compromised in a hacking incident behind a viral post calling Israel a “terrorist state.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: China hacked Treasury Department earlier this month: Officials
item.description: The bad actor overrode security measures and remotely accessed workstations and "certain unclassified documents" from users.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Small businesses brace themselves for potentially disruptive TikTok ban
item.description: A looming TikTok ban could affect the millions of small businesses that use the short-video social media app to grow their business.
item.dc:creator: Mae Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The long-term impacts of AI on networking
item.description: AI is either going to spawn a golden age of profits and benefits or kill us all off, according to popular viewpoints on the subject. The latter view has been at least somewhat discredited; if you yell “AI” in a crowded theater these days, hardly anyone runs for the exits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Multitenant data management with TiDB
item.description: In a previous article, we explored various approaches to managing data for multitenant applications. We examined the benefits, compromises and limitations of different deployment models, including share-nothing, share-everything, and hybrid.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 hot programming trends
item.description: Tides ebb and flow. Pendulums swing. Ideas come and go, especially in programming, where the technologies and techniques for creating software continuously evolve. Some programming languages, libraries, platforms, and tools become hot and irresistible.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, NewsNation

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk causes uproar by backing Germany’s far-right party AfD
item.description: The Tesla Motors CEO also wrote that his investment in Germany gave him the right to comment on the country’s condition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Stuck behind a VPN wall? Let’s find a way around it
item.description: A virtual private network (VPN) allows computer and smartphone users to mask an IP and create a secure, encrypted connection between a device and the internet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Android 15's new Private Space feature puts an end to app snooping
item.description: Android 15's latest feature, Private Space, is like a private vault helping to keep your personal or sensitive apps away from prying eyes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tech investor Cathie Wood on how Trump can boost the U.S. economy
item.description: U.S. tech investor Cathie Wood is calling on Donald Trump’s incoming administration to boost economic growth and policy certainty by backdating promised corporate and personal tax cuts to Jan. 1, 2025, she told Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors
item.description: Learn how rich text editors help government agencies ensure compliance with data security, accessibility, and document standardization. The post Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Scammers are targeting teens with these nasty tricks
item.description: Cybercriminals have more ways than ever to target teenagers; here are some of the most widespread scams and tricks to watch in order to keep your loved ones safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
